rule: hit iff kw>=2 OR sem>=0.7 OR func>=0.7; total_hits>=1 -> FAIL

results.reason: Source-domain Wikipedia page on totem poles. Matches 'tlingit totem pole' content word but is the declared source, not an LLM functional comparator. No LLM mechanism.

NEXUSSUM uses hierarchical LLM agents producing K-stacked composite outputs from long-form text. Hierarchical processing with segment composition overlaps strongly with K-stacked composite + per-segment processing kernel; sem >= 0.7 and func >= 0.7.

PipeSpec is hierarchical staged decoding across multiple models with stages/segments and inherited drafts -- overlaps with bottom-up segment decoder + per-segment processing kernel; sem and func both >= 0.7.
Speculative Streaming uses multi-stream attention for decoding speed; lacks bottom-up ordering and per-segment grounded disambiguation; under both 0.7 thresholds.
PECAN DAG-of-Information-Points with multi-predecessor info points functionally matches per-segment context inheritance + composite structured output; high sem and func.
Multi-agent character-conditioned story writing has character ownership analog but forward-time generation, no bottom-up reverse order or grounded disambiguation; under both thresholds.

COLLABSTORY survey describes agents passing segments + hierarchical story decomposition into K composite segments; matches K-stacked composite + per-segment context inheritance functionally; sem and func >= 0.7.
Hierarchical LLM-agent framework decomposing narrative into action segments with inherited context overlaps K-stacked composite + per-segment context inheritance kernel; sem and func at threshold.

fail_reason: 5 prior-art hits found (NEXUSSUM, PipeSpec, PECAN, COLLABSTORY survey, Narrative-to-Action). The candidate's K-stacked hierarchical composite generation with per-segment context inheritance is well-covered by 2025 hierarchical LLM-agent literature; the bottom-up reverse-order twist alone is insufficient differentiation. total_hits >= 1 triggers FAIL.
